summary: A valid IPv4 address has exactly four octets, three separators, and at most fifteen characters, so legal inputs cannot provide a fourfold scalable workload.

workload_bound.source_constraint: address is one syntactically valid IPv4 address

replacement_checks.evidence: Four decimal octets of at most three digits plus three dots give length at most fifteen.
Authored cases cover shortest and longest octets, zeros, repeated digits, and every separator position.
